title: Continuous Time Mediation
text: Computations research performed Pennsylvania State University’s Institute Computational Data Sciences’ Roar supercomputer. See .sim/README.md scripts .sim folder GitHub repository details simulation performed.

what: Delta Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — DeltaXMY
title: Delta Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — DeltaXMY
text: function generates delta method confidence intervals mediation model \(X \M \Y\).

title: Delta Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — DeltaXMY
Delta Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — DeltaXMY
text: phi_hat R object. Output PhiHat() function. delta_t Numeric vector. Vector time intervals.

title: Delta Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — DeltaXMY
code: if (FALSE) { set.seed(42) library(dynr) sim <- GenData(n = 50) data <- RandomMeasurement(sim) fit <- FitDynr(data) phi_hat <- PhiHat(fit) ci <- DeltaXMY(phi_hat) plot(ci) }

title: Monte Carlo Method Confidence Intervals for X-M-Y — MCXMY
code: if (FALSE) { set.seed(42) library(dynr) sim <- GenData(n = 50) data <- RandomMeasurement(sim) fit <- FitDynr(data) phi_hat <- PhiHat(fit) ci <- MCXMY(phi_hat) plot(ci) }

title: Simulation Replication — Sim
text: taskid Positive integer. Task ID. repid Positive integer. Replication ID. output_folder Character string. Output folder. overwrite Logical. Overwrite existing output output_folder. integrity Logical. integrity = TRUE, check output file integrity overwrite = FALSE. params_taskid Data frame single row. Simulation parameters specific taskid. delta_t Numeric vector. Vector time intervals. R Positive integer. Number random drift matrices generate.
